doc.title: Stock options — grant to exercise (or expiration)
doc.event: On January 1, Cole Corp. grants employees 10,000 stock options (grant-date fair value $6 each; $20 strike; $5 par) vesting over a 2-year service period. Later, 6,000 options are exercised and 4,000 expire. Prepare the annual expense, exercise, and expiration entries.

lines.label: 60,000 ÷ 2 years
lines.why: Total compensation = options × GRANT-date FV (10,000 × $6), spread straight-line over the service period.
lines.trap: Measured ONCE at grant. The stock price can double or crater afterward — the expense never remeasures.

lines.label: equity accrues
lines.why: The credit builds equity as the employees earn it — options are equity instruments from grant.
lines.trap: Not a liability: the company will deliver SHARES, not cash. (Cash-settled SARs are the liability cousin — different animal.)

lines.label: 6,000 × $20 strike
lines.trap: The strike — not the market price on exercise day — sets the cash.

lines.label: their share of the 60,000
lines.why: The equity built during vesting rides along: 6,000/10,000 × 60,000 = 36,000 transfers into the permanent stock accounts.
lines.trap: Proportional — only the EXERCISED options' slice moves; the rest stays for the remaining options' fate.

lines.label: 6,000 × $5 par
lines.trap: Par of the shares issued, nothing to do with the strike or the FV.

lines.account: Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Common
lines.label: the plug
lines.trap: Both sources of value (cash paid + compensation earned) end up in contributed capital — no income effect at exercise.

lines.why: The lapsed options' slice leaves the options account…
lines.trap: …but the EXPENSE recognized during the service period is NEVER reversed. The employees rendered the service; the accounting stands.

lines.label: reclass, not reversal
lines.why: Equity just changes labels — total paid-in capital is untouched.
lines.trap: Contrast with RESTRICTED STOCK forfeiture, which DOES reverse expense. Vested-but-unexercised ≠ forfeited-before-earning. This pairing is a classic exam discriminator.

memorize.body: Options: total comp = options × grant-date FV, straight-line over service. Exercise: cash (strike) + options-PIC slice in → common par + PIC. Expiration: reclass PIC, never reverse expense.
Grant-date FV is a photograph, not a video — snapped once, never retaken, no matter what the stock does after.
Expiration ≠ forfeiture: expiration (vested, unexercised) reclasses equity and keeps the expense; forfeiture (service condition failed) reverses expense. Read which one the problem describes.

questions.prompt: Options carrying 24,000 of Paid-in Capital — Stock Options expire unexercised. Compensation expense is…

distractors.feedback: No reversal — the service was rendered and the grant-date measurement stands. Expiration only RECLASSES the 24,000 from PIC—Stock Options to PIC—Expired Stock Options. (Reversal is the FORFEITURE rule, for restricted stock or unvested awards.)

accountBank.account: Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Common

build.scaffold: Three legs, one photograph: the grant-date FV. It expenses over service, rides along at exercise (proportionally), and re-labels at expiration. Cash appears only at exercise, and only at the strike.
